title: I Am Older. I Am Wiser. Both Are Gifts.
subtitle: An affirmation for reclaiming age as an asset rather than a loss
excerpt: A three-part affirmation for women who feel the cultural pressure to apologise for or minimise their age. Holds both realities: the loss and the gain that comes with getting older.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education, drawing on life-stage literature and the positive-ageing tradition.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you catch yourself wishing you were younger, apologising for your age, or feeling the birthday as a small loss rather than an arrival.

content.text: A three-statement affirmation for the birthday, the milestone, the quiet morning when the mirror shows a year that passed. Not a statement that age doesn't matter. A statement that age matters in a good way.
Say each statement aloud, slowly, once in the morning. Three statements. Full stop after each.
The short pause between each statement is part of the practice. Let the first one land before you move to the second. The three together build something the single-sentence version misses.

Written for homemade.education, drawing on positive-ageing literature and the life-stage tradition that names the second half of life as a distinct season with its own gifts. The three-statement form is adapted from affirmation practice in the energy-statement lineage.
